english: pf. bṡer (bsher), to compare, to confront Cs.

crystal, glass Dzl. and elsewh.; acc. to Stg. the moon also consists of such crystal Cs.: raṅ-ṡel (rang shel) native crystal, bżú-ṡel (bzhu shel) artificial crystal, glass; mán-ṡel (man shel) Pth. prob. = ṡel (shel); spos-ṡėl (spos shel) amber; me-ṡėl (me shel) burning-glass, ču-ṡėl (chu shel) चन्द्रकान्त a fabulous magic stone supposed to have the power of producing water or even rain. Comp. ṡel-kór (shel skor) or -pór (-phor) a tumbler. — ṡel-dkár (shel dkar) = ṡel (shel) Glr. — ṡel-káṅ (shel khang) glass-works, glass-manufactory Schr. — ṡel-sgóṅ (shel sgong) globe of glass Mil. — ṡel-sgó (shel sgo) glass-door. — ṡel-rdó (shel rdo) crystal. — ṡel-snód (shel snod), ṡel-spyád (shel spyad) a crystal or glass vessel. — ṡel-prė́ṅ (shel phreng) a string of glass-beads. — ṡel-búm (shel bum) glass-bottle. — ṡel-míg (shel mig) spectacles, spy-glass, telescope.

(synon. rig-pa (rig pa), resp. mkyén-pa (mkhyen pa)) I. vb., 1. to know, perceive, apprehend, bzaṅ-bar ṡes-pa (bzang bar shes pa) to find, to know a thing to be good Glr.; brtágs-na mi ṡes (brtags na mi shes) when (the soul) is searched for, it is not to be perceived or apprehended Mil.; ṡes-pai blo (shes pa'i blo) ingenium sapiens Dzl.; mi-ṡes-pa-dag (mi shes pa dag) those who do not care for knowing (a thing) Dzl.; su ṡes (su shes) B., C., *či ṡe* (ci she) W. (like the Hindi क्या जाने) who can tell? may be; čiaṅ mi ṡė-k'an (ci ang mi she mkhan) a know-nothing, ignoramus, dunce; *k'o-ráṅ mā ṡé-k'an čén-mo żig tsor dug* (kho rang ma she mkhan chen mo zhig tshor dug) W. he is said to be an extremely clever (learned etc.) man; mi-ṡes dgu ṡes-pa (mi shes dgu shes pa) knowing (even) the unknown things, knowing every thing Thgy.; čaṅ-ṡes (cang shes) id.; *ṅa-ráṅ toṅ ṡe* (nga rang mthong she) W. I know it from having seen it; ṡes-par gyur (shes par gyur) 1. he will know. 2. he comes to know, he learns; ṡes-par gyis ṡig (shes par gyis shig) 1. know! 2. let it be known! ṡes-bżin-du (shes bzhin du) knowing, knowingly, with (my) knowledge; ṅó-ṡes-pa (ngo shes pa) = ṡes-pa (shes pa), yet cf. sub ṅo (ngo). — 2. to understand, = gó-ba (go ba), don (don) the sense Glr.; ṅas rtsis ṡes (ngas rtsis shes) I understand mathematics; to be able, in a general sense, also physically: *g'aṅ ṡe-pa* (gang she pa) C. to one's best ability, to the utmost of one's power (= ji nus-kyis (ji nus kyis) B., *či túb-k'an* (ci thub mkhan) W.); k'rág-gi goṅ gul ṡes-pa (khrag gi gong gul shes pa) a clot of blood that could only quiver (though, in fact, a human being) Glr.; esp. with a negative: smra mi ṡes-pa (smra mi shes pa) not being able to speak, dgye dgu mi ṡes (dgye dgu mi shes) they cannot be bent or curved Med. — 3. to be convinced, to be of opinion, to think, sú-la yaṅ mdzá-bor ma ṡes (su la yang mdza' bor ma shes) do not think anybody to be your friend! II. sbst. (= rig-pa (rig pa)) 1. the knowing (about a thing), knowledge. — 2. science, learning, ṡes-pa-la zóg-tu ltá-ba (shes pa la zog tu lta ba) to look upon science as a (sort of) cheating. — 3. intellectual power, intelligence, ṡes-pa t'ibs (shes pa thibs) the intellect (of infants) is still very weak Lt., gsal (gsal) is clear Pth. — 4. the soul or spirit, separate from the body Thgy., Mil. Comp. *ṡe-gyá* (she rgya) talent(?) C., W. — *ṡe-gyí'* (she rgyi) character C., W., *ṡe-gyí' ṅẹ́m-pa* (she rgyi ngan pa) a bad character. — ṡes-dód (shes 'dod) desire of knowledge, curiosity of mind Mil. ṡes-ldan (shes ldan), ṡes-blo-ldán-pa (shes blo ldan pa) 1. knowing, rich in wisdom. 2. very learned Sir! — ṡes-po (shes po), ṡes-pa-po (shes pa po) one that knows or understands, a knower Cs. — ṡes-bya (shes bya) 1. what may be known or ought to be known, ṡes-bya kun (shes bya kun) every thing worth knowing, all the sciences. 2. knowing, conscious, wilful? ṡes-byai sgrib-pa (shes bya'i sgrib pa) contamination by wilful sins Do.? — ṡes-byed (shes byed) that which knows, the understanding. — ṡes-bżin (shes bzhin) consciousness (v. above ṡes-bżin-du (shes bzhin du)), dran-pa daṅ ṡes-bżin-čan yin-te (dran pa dang shes bzhin can yin te) Gyatch. IV, 14 (cf. Burn. II, 806, 5); ṡes-bżin (shes bzhin) may, accordingly, be used for 'conscience' in a christian sense. — ṡes-yoṅ (shes yongs) Ts. = ṡes-rgya (shes rgya). — ṡes-ráb (shes rab) (प्रज्ञा) 1. 'great knowledge', wisdom, intelligence, understanding, talent, ṡes-rab če-ba (shes rab che ba) very talented, gifted (e.g. a boy) Mil.; ṡes-ráb daṅ ldán-pa (shes rab dang ldan pa) id.; ṡes-rab-spyan (shes rab spyan) the (mystic) eye of wisdom Schl. p. 210. — ṡes-rab-rtswa (shes rab rtswa) Taraxacum, dandelion, also used as food. — ṡes-ráb-kyi pá-rol-tu pyín-pa (shes rab kyi pha rol tu phyin pa), प्रज्ञापारमिता, the having arrived at the other side of wisdom, n. of that section of the Kangyur which treats of philosophical matters.

(Cs.: ṡó-mo (sho mo)) I. die, dice, ṡo rgyáb-pa (sho rgyab pa) to dice, ṡo rtsé-ba (sho rtse ba) to play at dice, ₒgyéd-pa ('gyed pa) (Sch. kyé-żig (kye zhig) as imp.) id.? — ṡo rgyál-ba (sho rgyal ba) or pám-pa (pham pa) to win or lose at playing;
